intro: Breynia retroversa, also known as the false nettle vine, is a tropical plant species native to the Pacific Islands and Australia, characterized by its glossy leaves and clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties.
